macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Posts Fifth Consecutive Weekly Loss Amid Middle East Conflict
items.summary: The S&P 500 fell 2% this week, marking its first five-straight weekly losses since 2022, as markets grapple with elevated oil prices and uncertainty from the Middle East conflict. The index is pacing for its worst month in a year despite strong April earnings momentum.

items.title: Oil Prices Surge to $112/Barrel, Testing Central Banks' Inflation Control
items.summary: Brent crude climbed to $112 per barrel amid Middle East tensions, with U.S. 10-year Treasury yields rising to 4.43%. Central banks face a critical question: whether policy rates can keep pace with rising inflation if energy prices don't decline soon.

items.title: Fed Maintains Rates at 3.5-3.75% Despite Elevated Inflation and Conflict Uncertainty
items.summary: The Federal Reserve held its target federal funds rate steady at its recent meeting, citing the need to balance risks to employment and price stability amid elevated inflation, mixed labor market signals, and Middle East conflict uncertainty. Overall inflation rose to 3.5% in March, above the Fed's 2% target.
items.source: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

items.title: Core Inflation Broadens as Service Sector Posts Largest Monthly Gains Since Mid-2025
items.summary: Core inflation is rising across multiple categories including goods and services, with service-sector inflation posting its largest monthly gains since mid-2025 and housing prices climbing. Central banks globally are signaling hawkish stances in response.

items.title: Palantir Beats Earnings Estimates but Stock Shrugs Off Strong Results
items.summary: Palantir (PLTR) exceeded analyst expectations by 18% on EPS and 6% on revenue, yet shares settled little-changed in after-hours trading, suggesting waning conviction in the AI growth narrative as earnings tailwinds fade.

items.title: RBA Delivers Third Consecutive Rate Hike Amid War-Related Inflation Pressures
items.summary: The Reserve Bank of Australia delivered its third consecutive 25 basis point rate increase this year, with a fourth hike expected before the end of 2026, as central banks globally tighten policy in response to Middle East conflict-driven inflation.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Bitcoin Breaks Above $80,000 for First Time Since January
items.summary: Bitcoin has surpassed the $80,000 mark, reaching $80,324, marking its first touch of this level since January. The move follows a relief rally from a $58,000 bottom, with technical indicators showing bullish momentum as the MACD flipped back to bullish after briefly turning bearish on Friday.

items.title: DTCC Plans Tokenized Securities Platform with July Pilot
items.summary: Wall Street's Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC) is planning to launch a tokenized securities platform, with a pilot program scheduled for July and a full launch expected in October.

items.title: Western Union Launches USDPT Stablecoin on Solana
items.summary: Western Union has launched its USDPT stablecoin on the Solana blockchain, expanding traditional financial services into the crypto ecosystem.

items.title: Ethereum Poised for Major Update with Glamsterdam
items.summary: Ethereum is preparing a significant update called Glamsterdam that could increase transactions per second by 300% and raise the gas limit from 60 million to 200 million, with Ethereum breaking out of a 7-month trend line.

items.title: Clarity Act Expected to Pass This Month, Boosting Crypto Market
items.summary: A compromise Clarity Act for cryptocurrency regulation is expected to be pushed through sometime in May 2026, with anticipation of the legislation driving institutional investment into crypto markets and supporting the current rally.

items.title: Banks Accumulating XRP Amid Retail Selloff
items.summary: Institutional data suggests major banks including Goldman Sachs are accumulating XRP while retail sentiment remains depressed, with trading volume surging 82% despite stagnant price action, indicating a potential supply shock.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Google Gemini Receives Major Update with Enhanced Reasoning Capabilities
items.summary: Google has released a significant update to Gemini featuring improved reasoning capabilities, performance enhancements, and advanced AI functionality that experts say could transform how users interact with AI systems.

items.title: AI-Assisted Protocol Exploit Steals 11 Bitcoin from Bisq Decentralized Exchange
items.summary: The Bisq decentralized exchange was compromised in what appears to be an AI-assisted attack, resulting in the theft of 11 Bitcoin and highlighting new security vulnerabilities in self-custody ecosystems.

items.title: IBM Marks Decade of Quantum Computing on the Cloud
items.summary: IBM reflects on ten years since making quantum computers accessible via cloud infrastructure, including a 2023 Nature publication demonstrating quantum utility with accurate computational results.

items.title: Understanding How Large Language Models Actually Work: Beyond the Hype
items.summary: A comprehensive explanation of how LLMs like ChatGPT, Claude, and Gemini function, covering tokens, training, hallucinations, context windows, and why these systems can be both useful and unreliable.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Subnautica 2 Early Access Release Date Announced
items.summary: Unknown Worlds Entertainment has revealed that Subnautica 2, the most wishlisted game on Steam, will enter early access on May 14, 2026—less than two weeks away.

items.title: Pragmata Sequel in Development Following Game's Success
items.summary: Capcom is reportedly already considering a follow-up to Pragmata after the game's incredible commercial and critical success.

items.title: SOS Roguelike Releases to Critical Acclaim
items.summary: SOS launched recently with nearly 90% positive reviews on Metacritic, though it remains a PlayStation exclusive with no announced plans for Xbox or PC ports.

items.title: The Blood of Dawn Walker Gameplay and Release Date Revealed
items.summary: A new 13-minute gameplay deep dive was released for The Blood of Dawn Walker, which will launch on PC, Xbox, and PlayStation.

items.title: Vitality Continues Dominance in CS:GO Esports
items.summary: Team Vitality has set multiple records in 2026, including 18 consecutive series wins, 22 consecutive playoff map wins, and 20 consecutive top-four finishes at HLTV major events.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: GameStop Submits $56 Billion Offer to Acquire eBay
items.summary: GameStop has reportedly submitted a $56 billion offer to buy eBay, with the deal potentially coming as soon as this month according to The Wall Street Journal.

items.title: Apple Explores Using Intel and Samsung for US Chip Production
items.summary: Apple held exploratory talks with Intel and visited a Samsung plant in Texas to consider producing core chips for its devices domestically.

items.title: Lattice Semiconductor to Acquire AMI for $1.65 Billion
items.summary: Lattice Semiconductor has agreed to buy Georgia-based AMI, a provider of firmware and infrastructure manageability for cloud and AI, in a $1.65 billion cash and stock deal.

items.title: iOS 26.5 Adds End-to-End Encryption for RCS Messages
items.summary: Apple's upcoming iOS 26.5 update will introduce end-to-end encryption for RCS messages exchanged between iPhone and Android devices.

items.title: Ask.com Shuts Down, Ending Era of Early Search Butler
items.summary: Ask.com, known for its original AI-powered search butler, has officially shut down.

items.title: Elon Musk Settles SEC Dispute Over Twitter Investment for $1.5 Million
items.summary: Elon Musk has settled a years-long dispute with the SEC for $1.5 million regarding his Twitter investment without admitting wrongdoing.
